title: Rules engine: extension_to_language is 40-arm match with NO test coverage per language
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension**: Structural Modularity (OCP violation) + Testability Coverage

**Location**: src/rules.rs:11-56 (extension_to_language), src/rules.rs:155-198 (known_languages array)

**Violation**: `extension_to_language` is a 45-arm match statement and `known_languages` is a parallel hardcoded array of 40 strings. These two lists MUST stay in sync but nothing enforces it. Adding a new language requires changes in both places — a maintainer-coupling trap. The RulesEngine would silently fail to dispatch rules to a newly added extension if only one of the two tables is updated.

Cyclomatic complexity of extension_to_language is >40, violating the stated threshold of 15 in the CLAUDE.md guidelines.

Tests (rules.rs:348-399) only cover rs/py/ts — the other 37 mappings are entirely untested. The function is an OCP violation: every new language requires editing the core function body.

**Mandated Refactor**:
1. Consolidate into a single `const LANGUAGES: &[(&str, &[&str])] = &[("rust", &["rs"]), ("python", &["py", "pyw"]), ...]` table.
2. Derive extension_to_language and known_languages from the same table.
3. Add a compile-time test or build.rs assertion that table entries have kebab-case names and at least one extension.
4. Expose LANGUAGES as pub(crate) so tests can iterate and assert bidirectional consistency.
5. Consider using the `tree-sitter-language-registry` crate or similar rather than reinventing.

Note: file naming collision between src/rules.rs (rules engine) and src/config/guardrails.rs / src/guardrails.rs is also confusing — top-level rules.rs is unrelated to linting rules and should be renamed context_rules.rs or similar.
